project.name: zca-desktop
project.summary: Cross-platform Zalo desktop client (Tauri v2 + Rust) built on the zca-rust unofficial API. Supports multiple accounts logged in simultaneously and coexistence with the user's other Zalo devices. agent-harness-kit is the dev control center (model routing + feature tracking + review gates).

in: Multi-account SessionManager + account switcher + per-account routing

risks.title: Unofficial API / ToS + account-ban risk
risks.mitigation: Treat as personal-use tool; rate-limit; surface warnings; no automation that looks like spam.

risks.title: Cookie/session expiry invalidates login
risks.mitigation: Detect 401/expiry, mark account re-auth-needed, prompt re-import.

risks.title: Multi-device same-account conflicts (imei/session)
risks.mitigation: Each account = distinct exported session; verify coexistence (device-coexistence feature) before claiming support.

risks.title: Secret handling (cookies are bearer tokens)
risks.mitigation: OS keychain via keyring; never log/echo cookie values; gitignore cred files.

risks.title: zca-rust endpoints only 43/149 verified live
risks.mitigation: Build on verified endpoints first; gate unverified behind live-test evidence.

risks.title: Dev affordances pending replacement: .zalo-cred.json file loader (-> secure-cred-store OS keychain) and UI placeholder tabs/icons. RESOLVED by session-manager: the in-command session store is now the session/ SessionManager layer, and per-account send throttling (800ms) is in place (ban risk r1 mitigated).

decisions.title: Rich message events mutate cached message rows: reactions update reaction_icon, undo marks deleted, quote/link JSON is persisted for restart hydration; text/quote sends route through SessionManager throttle with explicit ThreadKind.

decisions.summary: Frontend chat UI (shadcn-svelte 3-pane) reaches the Rust core ONLY via Tauri invoke/listen against command/; conversations + contacts are derived from live IPC (zalo://message, list_contacts) with no mock/sample data.
decisions.why: Keeps the ADR-0003 ui/core boundary clean and avoids fixtures that drift from real behavior.

decisions.summary: Live Zalo features are verified with #[ignore] cargo tests that read .zalo-cred.json, run locally (bash tool runs on the user's Mac, normal IP); evidence captures only non-secret diagnostics and every smoke sidecar is scanned for token/PII leakage before commit.
decisions.why: Live login/listen/send/contacts need a real session; this pattern proves them without leaking bearer tokens into the committed evidence trail.

decisions.summary: Credentials are read by the Rust core from .zalo-cred.json (dev) / OS keychain (planned); they MUST never enter the webview/UI. UI uses file-backed commands + non-secret DTOs only.
decisions.why: Pasting the imei+cookie+userAgent bearer token into the webview DOM leaks it; the core-read pattern keeps the token out of the UI.

decisions.title: Tauri v2 + Rust core hosts zca-rust sessions; webview = UI
zca-rust consumed as git dependency (not crates.io)
Multi-account = many in-process API+Listener sessions in a SessionManager
Credentials imported as ZaloDataExtractor JSON; stored in OS keychain
Cross-install sync relay is out of MVP (stretch, ADR-gated)
zca-rust consumed as a pinned git rev (08698e1c), rustls TLS — no native bindings, no new ADR

Local persistence (ADR-0005): bundled SQLite (rusqlite) for accounts/threads/messages; credential triple AES-256-GCM encrypted with a master key held in the OS keychain (keyring), never plaintext on disk; session auto-restore on startup

Harness honesty rule: a merged feature is only flipped passes:true when its proof is runnable in-environment (message-cache restart smoke offline; secure-cred-store keychain smoke on this Mac). Features needing a human/device action (qr-login real phone scan; session-restore GUI relaunch) stay passes:false and are tracked as needs-human failure records rather than fake-passed.

Device-only proofs (qr-login real phone scan, session-restore relaunch) are validated by reading the core log and captured as a non-secret log artifact, with an attested grep asserting the success marker — satisfying high-risk strict attestation without a fake cargo run. qr-login + session-restore are now passes:true; all 4 merged features reconciled and readiness is green.
